meta.title: BST Domain Map
meta.description: All domains that BST touches, with theorem counts from the AC graph, key results, and cross-references. BST is not a physics theory that also does math — it is a geometric theory where physics, chemistry, biology, and mathematics are all consequences of D_IV^5.

domains.name: Foundations (AC Program)
domains.description: The AC (Algebraic Complexity) program — BST's mathematical backbone. Complexity theory, proof complexity, P!=NP approach, depth ceilings, linearization.
key_results: T1: AC Dichotomy — tractable = zero hidden information
T35: AC(0) chain rule + BSW + expansion
T96: Depth Reduction — composition with definitions is free
T421: Depth-1 Ceiling under Casey strict

domains.name: BST Core Physics
domains.description: The physical core of BST — D_IV^5 geometry, Bergman kernel, Wyler formula, mass derivations, SM from geometry.

domains.name: Biology
domains.description: Genetic code, evolution, protein structure, neural oscillations — all from D_IV^5. Proton and DNA are siblings: each level expresses subset of five integers.

domains.name: Observer Science
domains.description: Observer physics, consciousness, cooperation, information processing, CI persistence. The observer is not external to the geometry — it IS geometry observing itself.

domains.name: Number Theory
domains.description: Riemann Hypothesis, prime distribution, L-functions, BSD conjecture. BST connects number theory to physics via D_IV^5 spectral structure.

domains.name: Chemical Physics
domains.description: Bond angles, molecular geometry, water properties, material constants. Chemistry is physics at the n_C=5 level.

domains.name: Cosmology
domains.description: Lambda-CDM parameters, CMB, dark energy/matter, Hubble constant, cosmic age, MOND — all derived from five integers.

domains.name: Proof Complexity
domains.description: AC depth ceilings, proof compression, Millennium problem proofs as AC graph operations.

domains.name: Topology
domains.description: Topological foundations of BST — winding numbers, Hopf fibrations, Z_3 confinement, four-color theorem.

domains.name: Thermodynamics
domains.description: Entropy, phase transitions, interstasis hypothesis, arrow of time, Casimir effects.

domains.name: Linearization Program
domains.description: Standing order: linearize every mathematical area. All six Millennium problems have linearization theorems. Linearization COMPLETE (T811).

domains.name: Information Theory
domains.description: Shannon capacity, channel coding, information bounds. BST is fundamentally an information theory — physics is what information does on geometry.
key_results: T1: AC Dichotomy (tractable = zero hidden info)

domains.name: Differential Geometry
domains.description: Hodge conjecture approach, Kahler geometry, Bergman spaces, spectral geometry of D_IV^5.

domains.name: Quantum Mechanics
domains.description: Measurement problem dissolved, no-cloning, Born rule, quantum foundations from D_IV^5 contact graph.

domains.name: Graph Theory
domains.description: Contact graph structure, theorem graph properties, four-color, spectral graph theory.

domains.name: Nuclear Physics
domains.description: Magic numbers, binding energies, proton stability, neutron lifetime — all from kappa_ls = 6/5 and Z_3 topology.
key_results: T305: Magic numbers from kappa_ls = C_2/n_C = 6/5

domains.name: Fluid Mechanics / Turbulence
domains.description: Navier-Stokes, Kolmogorov turbulence, She-Leveque, percolation. K41 exponent 5/3 = n_C/N_c.
key_results: T818: Kolmogorov 5/3 = n_C/N_c and She-Leveque as BST rationals

domains.name: Chemistry (Pure)
domains.description: Periodic table, crystal structures, molecular geometry at the pure chemistry level.
key_results: T740-T742: Crystal systems, Bravais lattices, point groups

domains.name: Analysis
domains.description: Spectral analysis, functional analysis, representation theory used in BST proofs.

domains.name: Condensed Matter
domains.description: FQHE, superconductivity, BCS, Debye temperatures. BST rationals appear in 76+ material property domains.
key_results: T813-T815: 26/28 FQHE fractions are BST rationals

domains.name: Relativity
domains.description: Black holes, Penrose singularity, EHT predictions, gravitational waves.

domains.name: Electromagnetism
domains.description: Maxwell equations from S^1 phase oscillation, Coulomb law, electromagnetic coupling.
key_results: T225: Coulomb's Law from phase comparison

domains.name: Coding Theory
domains.description: LDPC codes, error correction, Shannon capacity in the BST context.

domains.name: Quantum Field Theory
domains.description: Goldstone theorem, Yang-Mills mass gap, Wightman axioms, QED from BST.
key_results: Yang-Mills mass gap = 6*pi^5 * m_e = proton mass

domains.name: Algebra
domains.description: Algebraic independence, Lie algebras, representation theory — the algebraic substrate of BST.

domains.name: Classical Mechanics
domains.description: Newton's laws, Kepler orbits as consequences of the contact graph.

domains.name: Optics
domains.description: Snell's law, refractive indices, rainbow, diffraction from BST integers.

domains.name: Computational Complexity
domains.description: P vs NP approach, SAT, circuit complexity, Boolean function analysis.

domains.name: Computation
domains.description: Kolmogorov complexity, computability, halting problem connections.
key_results: T298: Kolmogorov Complexity in BST framework

domains.name: Probability
domains.description: Probabilistic methods, concentration inequalities, random graph theory.

domains.name: Signal Processing
domains.description: Heisenberg uncertainty, Fourier analysis, sampling theory from BST.

domains.name: Four-Color Theorem
domains.description: Computer-free proof of the four-color theorem via Forced Fan Lemma. 13 structural steps.
key_results: Forced Fan Lemma: Casey's mapmaker insight
PROVED — all 13 steps structural, zero computers

domains.name: Quantum Foundations
domains.description: Measurement problem, Born rule, decoherence — all dissolved by contact graph commitment.

domains.name: Music Theory
domains.description: Consonance hierarchy from BST rationals. Why octaves, fifths, and fourths sound good.
